app_info.subtitle: Use RemNote from ChatGPT
app_info.description: RemNote ChatGPT Bridge lets ChatGPT inspect a running RemNote workspace through a local companion server. It can read the focused Rem, read specific Rems, inspect bounded Rem trees, and create or append Rem content after the plugin permission flow allows it.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reports local bridge connection metadata and does not modify RemNote.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to public internet state or any external third-party system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only sends a small ping through the local plugin connection and returns the response.
justifications.open_world_justification: Operates only on the local bridge connection and does not publish or send external messages.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not alter RemNote content or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves plugin permission mode and focused Rem availability.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local plugin state and does not change public or external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or mutate RemNote data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only serializes the Rem currently focused in RemNote.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local private RemNote data and does not publish it to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not alter, overwrite, or delete RemNote content.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reads a specific Rem by ID and returns a serialized payload.
justifications.open_world_justification: Accesses the user's local RemNote data without changing public internet state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not modify or remove the requested Rem.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only reads a bounded Rem subtree with capped depth and serialized output.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads local RemNote content and does not write to external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not modify, replace, or delete any Rem in the subtree.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a new Rem in the user's RemNote workspace when the plugin permission flow allows it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish public content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates new content and does not delete or overwrite existing Rems.

justifications.read_only_justification: Appends a new child Rem under an existing Rem when the plugin permission flow allows it.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the user's private RemNote workspace and does not publish externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds child content without deleting or overwriting the target Rem.

test_cases.description: Check whether the local RemNote bridge is connected.
test_cases.user_prompt: Is my RemNote bridge connected right now?
test_cases.expected_output: Reports whether the local companion server currently has a RemNote plugin connection.

test_cases.description: Verify that ChatGPT can reach the running plugin through the bridge.
test_cases.user_prompt: Ping my RemNote plugin before reading anything.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a successful ping response from the local RemNote plugin connection.

test_cases.description: Read the Rem currently focused in RemNote.
test_cases.user_prompt: Read the Rem I am focused on in RemNote and summarize what it says.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the focused Rem content in structured form and summarizes it for the user.

test_cases.description: Read a known Rem by ID.
test_cases.user_prompt: Read Rem rem_12345 and tell me the front and back text.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the requested Rem content or a clear not-found error.

test_cases.user_prompt: Inspect Rem rem_12345 and its children up to depth 2.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a bounded serialized Rem tree with truncated markers if limits are reached.

test_cases.description: Create a new Rem from markdown after permission flow allows it.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a new Rem under rem_parent_1 with this markdown: ## Study plan\n- Review proofs\n- Practice recall.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates the Rem only after the plugin permission flow allows it and returns the new Rem ID.

test_cases.description: Append a child Rem after user approval.
test_cases.user_prompt: Append a child under my focused Rem with three follow-up questions.
test_cases.expected_output: Reads the focused Rem, proposes child markdown, waits for approval when required, and returns the appended child Rem ID.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated calendar requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What meetings do I have tomorrow morning?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it has no calendar workflow.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not expose direct OpenAI configuration inside RemNote.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Set my OpenAI API key in the RemNote plugin and switch to a different model.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should explain that the plugin does not store OpenAI API keys or choose models.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger destructive note replacement or deletion.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Delete this Rem and all of its children from RemNote.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not call a tool because destructive MCP tools are not exposed.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not attempt unbounded knowledge-base extraction.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Export my entire RemNote knowledge base into this chat.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should decline unbounded extraction and offer focused or bounded Rem reads instead.
